home.hero_subtitle: InfectoNET tracks genomic epidemiology of emerging and re-emerging pathogens worldwide — providing open, FAIR data for researchers and public health practitioners.
home.groups_subtitle: Select a pathogen group to explore genomic surveillance data, geographic distribution, and variant trends.

groups.respiratory_desc: Viruses primarily transmitted via respiratory droplets and aerosols
groups.vector_borne_desc: Viruses transmitted by arthropod vectors such as mosquitoes and ticks
groups.haemorrhagic_desc: Severe viral infections causing haemorrhagic manifestations
groups.zoonotic_desc: Viruses with animal reservoirs causing neurological or systemic disease
groups.childhood_desc: Viruses for which effective vaccines exist or affect primarily children
groups.retroviral_desc: RNA retroviruses including HIV-related, animal, and human endogenous retroviruses
groups.gastrointestinal_desc: Viruses and bacteria causing enteric infection, hepatitis, or gastrointestinal disease
groups.other_desc: Priority emerging pathogens and WHO Disease X candidates

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.no_data: No data available for this organism

about.title: About InfectoNET
about.mission_text: InfectoNET is an open-access global surveillance platform for infectious disease genomic epidemiology. We aggregate publicly available pathogen sequence data to track the emergence and spread of viruses and other pathogens worldwide.
about.fair_findable: Findable — Every dataset has a canonical, persistent URL.
about.fair_accessible: Accessible — Data is freely downloadable via our public API.
about.fair_interoperable: Interoperable — Export in JSON, CSV, or FASTA formats.
about.fair_reusable: Reusable — All data is openly licensed with full provenance metadata.
about.data_text: Sequence data is sourced from NCBI GenBank, GISAID, and Nextstrain, processed through standardised quality-control and metadata enrichment pipelines.

footer.tagline: Open infectious disease genomic surveillance for global health.
